sample_size.source_quote: Ultimately, 209 participants were included in the analysis.

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The median age of the patients was 83 years (74, 88)

mortality_predictors.source_quote: The AUC of the SIC score on day 1 was 0.545 (95% CI: 0.465–0.625; P = 0.277), with no statistical significance.
the AUCs of the SIC score on day 2, ΔSIC score-d2 (the difference between the SIC scores on days 1 and 2), the SIC score on day 4, and ΔSIC score-d4 were 0.601 (95% CI: 0.524–0.679; P < 0.05)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: ΔSIC score-d2 (change in SIC score from day 1 to day 2)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the AUCs of the SIC score on day 2, ΔSIC score-d2 (the difference between the SIC scores on days 1 and 2), the SIC score on day 4, and ΔSIC score-d4 were 0.601 (95% CI: 0.524–0.679; P < 0.05), 0.583 (95% CI: 0.504–0.662; P < 0.05)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: ΔSIC score-d4 (change in SIC score from day 1 to day 4)

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Forward stepwise multivariate Cox proportional hazards analysis
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, D-dimer, albumin, lactate levels, SOFA score, APACHE II score, septic shock, SIC on day 2 (Model 1) or SIC on day 4 (Model 2)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Multivariate Cox proportional hazards analysis showed that age, albumin levels, APACHE II score, and SIC on day 4 were independent predictors of 28-day mortality in patients with sepsis.

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Forward stepwise multivariate Cox proportional hazards analysis
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, D-dimer, albumin, lactate levels, SOFA score, APACHE II score, septic shock
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Multivariate Cox proportional hazards analysis showed that age, albumin levels, APACHE II score, and SIC on day 4 were independent predictors of 28-day mortality in patients with sepsis. However, the SIC on day 2 was not independently associated with patient outcomes
